64.reason: [Using post-increment (high++) passes the original value of 'high' to the left subtree and then increments it locally, but the incremented value is not used for the right subtree. This leads to incorrect height calculation. Should pass 'high + 1' instead.]

65.reason: [Same issue as above: post-increment does not correctly propagate increased height to the right subtree. Also, after the first call, 'high' is modified, which affects the second call incorrectly. Should use 'high + 1'.]
